tasks.title: SvelteKit 5 project scaffold
tasks.summary: Bare SvelteKit 5 project initialised with TypeScript strict mode and Vite.
tasks.description: Base SvelteKit project created with Svelte 5 rune mode enabled in svelte.config.js, TypeScript 5 in strict mode, and adapter-auto for environment-agnostic deployment. node_modules installed.
acceptance_criteria: npm run dev starts without errors
npm run check passes with 0 errors
svelte.config.js has runes: true
notes: Done. Base scaffold verified.

tasks.title: Database layer: Neon + Drizzle schema
tasks.summary: Neon PostgreSQL connection + Drizzle schema for notes, note_links, and Auth.js tables.
tasks.description: Drizzle ORM configured with @neondatabase/serverless HTTP transport. Schema defines notes (with tags[], aliases[], status enum, AI metadata), note_links (FK cascade), and the four Auth.js adapter tables. drizzle.config.ts at project root. Migration scripts added to package.json.
acceptance_criteria: npm run db:generate produces migration SQL
npm run db:migrate applies schema to Neon without errors
db client exports from src/lib/server/db/index.ts
notes: Done. Schema verified via svelte-check.

tasks.title: GitHub OAuth single-user auth
tasks.summary: Auth.js with GitHub provider gated to ALLOWED_GITHUB_USERNAME. Session stored in Neon via DrizzleAdapter.
tasks.description: SvelteKitAuth configured with GitHub provider. signIn callback checks profile.login against ALLOWED_GITHUB_USERNAME env var, returning false to block all other accounts. Auth guard in hooks.server.ts redirects unauthenticated requests to /auth/signin. Session exposed to all pages via +layout.server.ts.
acceptance_criteria: Visiting / without session redirects to /auth/signin
Signing in with the correct GitHub account succeeds
Signing in with any other GitHub account shows AccessDenied
Session persists across page reloads
notes: Done. Requires GITHUB_CLIENT_ID, GITHUB_CLIENT_SECRET, ALLOWED_GITHUB_USERNAME, AUTH_SECRET in .env.

tasks.title: D3 force-directed graph (home page)
tasks.summary: Interactive D3 force graph on home page. Nodes = notes, edges = note_links. Click node navigates to note detail.
tasks.description: ForceGraph.svelte component using D3 v7. Force simulation with forceLink, forceManyBody, forceCenter, forceCollide. Node colour by status. Pan/zoom via d3.zoom(). Drag to reposition. onMount/onDestroy lifecycle. Home page server load fetches all nodes and links.
acceptance_criteria: Home page renders D3 graph with no JS errors
Clicking a node navigates to /notes/{slug}
Pan and zoom work
Empty state shown when no notes exist
notes: Done. D3 drag type required explicit SVGGElement generic to satisfy TypeScript.

tasks.title: Note CRUD routes
tasks.summary: Full create / read / update / delete for notes. Wikilinks parsed on save to sync note_links.
tasks.description: Routes: /notes (list + delete action), /notes/new (create action with slug generation + wikilink extraction), /notes/[slug] (detail with rendered Markdown + related notes), /notes/[slug]/edit (update + delete actions with wikilink re-sync). NoteCard.svelte reusable component. slugify() and extractWikilinks() utilities.
acceptance_criteria: Create note inserts row, syncs note_links, redirects to detail
Edit note updates row, re-syncs note_links
Delete note cascades note_links
Detail page renders Markdown, resolves [[wikilinks]] to anchors
Broken wikilinks render as red strikethrough
notes: Done. Delete form must not be nested inside update form — separate <form> elements used.

tasks.title: Search by title, category, and tags
tasks.summary: GET /search with query params for title/category (ilike) and tags (arrayOverlaps).
tasks.description: Search page uses SvelteKit GET form actions — no JS required. Drizzle ilike() on title and category fields, arrayOverlaps() on tags array. Results rendered as NoteCard grid.
acceptance_criteria: Search by title fragment returns matching notes
Search by tag(s) returns notes with overlapping tags
Search by category returns matching notes
Empty query returns no results (not all notes)
notes: Done.

tasks.title: AI integration skeleton
tasks.summary: Stub files for Claude and ChatGPT API integration. Routes return 501.
tasks.description: src/lib/ai/claude.ts, chatgpt.ts, prompts.ts — stub functions throwing 'Not implemented'. API routes POST /api/ai/research and POST /api/ai/generate-note return {error: '...', status: 501}. Anthropic and OpenAI SDKs installed as devDependencies.
acceptance_criteria: POST /api/ai/research returns 501
POST /api/ai/generate-note returns 501
npm run check passes with 0 errors on AI files
notes: Skeleton only. See AI-001 and AI-002 for real implementation.

tasks.title: Theme token system and theme/accent toggle foundation
tasks.summary: Refactor shared styling tokens so Techy can support soft theme switching plus independent accent selection.
tasks.description: Extend src/app.css and the root layout to support the initial Night, Paper, and Mist themes plus Sky, Mint, Amber, and Rose accents. Theme and accent state should be applied at the app shell level, persisted locally, and exposed consistently to page chrome and D3 graph styling.
acceptance_criteria: App shell supports switching between Night, Paper, and Mist themes
Accent selection is independent from theme selection
Theme and accent choices persist across reloads
Shared tokens are available to graph, notes, and chat surfaces without one-off color definitions
notes: Keep the implementation token-driven so future UI work consumes the same theme system rather than reintroducing hardcoded page-specific colors.

tasks.title: Global shell and navigation refresh
tasks.summary: Refresh the shared shell and nav so the app feels soft, rounded, and spatial instead of card-heavy.
tasks.description: Rework the global layout and navigation to follow the style guide's soft observatory direction. The shell should support the new theme tokens, keep the graph page full-bleed where needed, and establish shared surface rules for notes and chat routes.
acceptance_criteria: Nav and shared shell use the new soft rounded visual language
Shared layout supports both full-bleed and bounded page surfaces cleanly
Theme and accent controls are available from the shared shell
The global shell does not make the home graph feel boxed in
notes: Treat pages as a few coordinated zones rather than many interchangeable cards.

tasks.title: Graph home experience refresh
tasks.summary: Refine the home page so the D3 graph reads as the hero with floating controls and soft overlays.
tasks.description: Update the graph page presentation to match the style guide: full-bleed canvas, minimal chrome, soft overlay controls, and restrained motion. The graph should feel central and immersive rather than contained inside a conventional app panel.
acceptance_criteria: Home page preserves a full-bleed graph environment
Controls, legends, and related UI appear as overlays or docks rather than a card wall
Graph styling respects the shared theme and accent tokens
Motion on the home page remains restrained and purposeful
notes: This is the visual anchor for the rest of the UI work.

tasks.title: Notes browse and detail experience refresh
tasks.summary: Refresh search, notes list, and note detail so they feel like a clean docs flow rather than a dashboard grid.
tasks.description: Rework the notes browsing and reading surfaces to follow the style guide. Search should feel like a soft command surface, result repetition should stay restrained, and note detail should read like a polished internal docs page with lightweight supporting metadata.
acceptance_criteria: Search and notes list feel like part of the same calm docs-oriented system
Full note view presents one dominant reading surface with lightweight supporting regions
Metadata, backlinks, and related-note regions support the note body without overwhelming it
The overall notes experience avoids defaulting to a wall of generic cards
notes: Keep repetition where it is honest, but avoid turning every section into the same boxed treatment.

tasks.title: Note authoring surface refresh
tasks.summary: Refresh create/edit note forms so authoring feels like part of the same soft docs environment.
tasks.description: Apply the style-guide direction to the note create and edit routes. The forms should feel clean and intentional, with soft controls, strong hierarchy, and enough structure for future AI assist and preview features without looking like a default form stack.
acceptance_criteria: Create and edit note pages share a coherent authoring layout
Form controls follow the shared theme tokens and soft rounded shape language
The pages leave room for future preview, autocomplete, and AI-assist features without a major layout rewrite
The authoring UI does not regress existing form semantics or server-action behavior
notes: Keep form behavior server-authoritative; this task is about presentation and layout, not changing validation flow.

tasks.title: Chat route scaffold and shell
tasks.summary: Create the dedicated /chat route and base conversation layout that future assistant features will use.
tasks.description: Add a first-pass chat page that follows current AI product conventions while staying inside Techy's soft observatory system. The route should provide the conversation column, composer area, and optional desktop context region so assistant features have a clear home instead of being bolted onto the graph page.
acceptance_criteria: GET /chat renders behind the existing auth guard
Chat page uses the shared shell, theme tokens, and rounded surface language
The layout includes a primary conversation area and a persistent composer region
The page has sensible empty and loading placeholder states for future assistant wiring
notes: Run npm run prepare after adding the route so generated $types stay current.

tasks.title: Deploy to Vercel
tasks.summary: Deploy the app to Vercel with all environment variables configured.
tasks.description: Push repo to GitHub, import into Vercel, configure all env vars (AUTH_SECRET, GITHUB_CLIENT_ID, GITHUB_CLIENT_SECRET, ALLOWED_GITHUB_USERNAME, DATABASE_URL). Update GitHub OAuth App callback URL to production domain.
acceptance_criteria: Production URL loads and redirects to /auth/signin
GitHub sign-in works on production domain
Notes persist across deployments (Neon DB connected)

tasks.title: Run DB migrations on Neon production
tasks.summary: Apply Drizzle migrations to the Neon production database.
tasks.description: With DATABASE_URL pointing to Neon, run npm run db:migrate to apply the generated SQL from the drizzle/ folder. Verify all tables exist via Neon console or Drizzle Studio.
acceptance_criteria: All tables exist in Neon: users, accounts, sessions, verification_tokens, notes, note_links
npm run db:studio can connect and browse tables
notes: Run locally with .env pointing to the production DATABASE_URL, or set env var inline.

tasks.title: Implement Claude API research endpoint
tasks.summary: Wire up POST /api/ai/research to call Claude with the RESEARCH_SYSTEM_PROMPT and return a Markdown note body.
tasks.description: Implement researchTopic() in src/lib/ai/claude.ts using @anthropic-ai/sdk. Update the /api/ai/research route to call it, passing the topic from the request body. Return {body: string} on success. Move @anthropic-ai/sdk from devDependencies to dependencies.
acceptance_criteria: POST /api/ai/research {topic: 'SvelteKit'} returns a non-empty Markdown string
Response includes ANTHROPIC_API_KEY usage
Errors handled gracefully (invalid key, rate limit)
notes: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY must be set in .env

tasks.title: Implement ChatGPT API research endpoint
tasks.summary: Wire up researchTopic() in chatgpt.ts using the OpenAI SDK.
tasks.description: Implement researchTopic() and generateNote() in src/lib/server/ai/chatgpt.ts using the openai SDK. Read OPENAI_API_KEY via $env/dynamic/private (NOT $env/static/private) — same pattern as claude.ts. Add a provider param to /api/ai/research and /api/ai/generate-note so callers can choose Claude vs GPT. Move openai from devDependencies to dependencies.
acceptance_criteria: POST /api/ai/research {topic: 'SvelteKit', provider: 'chatgpt'} returns a Markdown string
Provider defaults to 'claude' when not specified
notes: OPENAI_API_KEY must be set in .env

tasks.title: AI research button on New Note page
tasks.summary: Add a 'Research with AI' button to the create-note form that calls /api/ai/research and populates the body textarea.
tasks.description: On /notes/new, add a button next to the title field. On click, POST to /api/ai/research with the current title value, then populate the body textarea with the returned Markdown. Show a loading spinner during the request. Set ai_generated=true, ai_model, ai_prompt as hidden fields.
acceptance_criteria: Button disabled until a title is entered
Clicking button shows loading state
Body textarea populated with AI response
Submitted note has ai_generated=true in the database
notes: Requires client-side fetch — break out of pure form-action pattern for this feature.

tasks.title: Graph category/status filter panel
tasks.summary: Add a filter panel to the home graph to show/hide nodes by category or status.
tasks.description: Add an overlay panel (bottom-left or side drawer) to ForceGraph.svelte with checkboxes for each category and status value. Filtering hides nodes and their connected edges without re-running the simulation. Node data passed from +page.server.ts already includes category and status.
acceptance_criteria: Toggling a category hides all nodes in that category
Toggling a status hides all nodes with that status
Filtered nodes' connected edges also hide
Filter state is local (not persisted)

tasks.title: Markdown preview pane in note editor
tasks.summary: Split-pane editor: textarea on the left, live Markdown preview on the right.
tasks.description: Add a client-side preview pane to the create/edit note forms. Use a Svelte reactive binding on the textarea to update the preview in real-time. Render via marked() client-side. Wikilinks in preview should be resolved against a list of note titles passed from the server.
acceptance_criteria: Typing in textarea updates preview without page reload
[[wikilinks]] render as styled spans (not anchor tags) in preview
Preview pane is optional / collapsible

tasks.title: Tag autocomplete in note form
tasks.summary: Show existing tags as suggestions when typing in the tags input field.
tasks.description: Load distinct tags from the DB in the create/edit server load function. Pass them to the page as a datalist or custom autocomplete component. User can select existing tags or type new ones.
acceptance_criteria: Typing in tags field shows dropdown of matching existing tags
Selecting a suggestion appends it to the input
New tags (not in suggestions) are also accepted
notes: HTML <datalist> is the simplest approach — no custom component needed.

tasks.title: Wikilink rename propagation
tasks.summary: When a note title is renamed, update [[wikilinks]] in all other notes' bodies that reference the old title.
tasks.description: Currently, renaming a note title does not update [[wikilinks]] referencing that title in other notes. On the update action in /notes/[slug]/edit/+page.server.ts, if the title has changed: find all notes whose body contains [[old title]], replace with [[new title]], update those notes and re-sync their note_links.
acceptance_criteria: Renaming a note title updates all [[oldtitle]] → [[newtitle]] in other notes
note_links table remains consistent after rename
No orphaned links after rename
notes: See ADR-007 for the known limitation context.

tasks.title: Implement AI generate-note endpoint
tasks.summary: Wire up POST /api/ai/generate-note to generate a full note draft and persist it to the database.
tasks.description: Implement the end-to-end generate-note flow so a topic prompt can produce a full note draft, insert a new note row, sync wikilinks, and return the created note metadata. Default provider can be Claude first; provider expansion can remain separate.
acceptance_criteria: POST /api/ai/generate-note returns a created note object with id, slug, and title
Generated note is inserted into the notes table
Generated note body is parsed for [[wikilinks]] and note_links are synced
Duplicate slug or title conflicts are handled gracefully
notes: This task closes the current gap where generate-note is mentioned in the PRD and API but not explicitly tracked on the workboard.

tasks.title: Provider selection across AI workflows
tasks.summary: Allow Claude vs ChatGPT selection for research and generate-note flows from the UI and API.
tasks.description: Add provider selection plumbing so the chosen model provider can be passed through research and generate-note requests. The API already anticipates a provider field; this task makes the behavior explicit in both server handlers and any relevant UI controls.
acceptance_criteria: Research flow can explicitly choose Claude or ChatGPT
Generate-note flow can explicitly choose Claude or ChatGPT
When no provider is selected, the system defaults to Claude
Unsupported provider values return a clear validation error
notes: Keep provider choice per-request rather than global configuration.

tasks.title: Persist AI provenance metadata consistently
tasks.summary: Ensure ai_generated, ai_model, and ai_prompt are populated consistently for AI-assisted note creation flows.
tasks.description: Audit and complete the metadata path so notes created or prefilled through AI-assisted flows persist provenance consistently. This includes create-form assistance, direct generate-note insertion, and any server-side fallback paths.
acceptance_criteria: AI-generated notes persist ai_generated=true
The model identifier is stored in ai_model
The originating prompt or topic request is stored in ai_prompt
Non-AI notes keep AI metadata unset or false
notes: The schema already includes these columns; this task is about consistent write-path behavior.

tasks.title: Graph colour-by-category toggle
tasks.summary: Add a graph display toggle so node colour can represent category instead of status.
tasks.description: Extend the home graph controls to switch the node colour mapping between status and category. Categories should use a stable, documented palette so the graph remains legible across reloads.
acceptance_criteria: Users can toggle between status colours and category colours
Category colours remain stable for the same category values
The default graph mode remains status colours
notes: If new category colours are introduced, document them in the design spec.

tasks.title: Graph node size proportional to link count
tasks.summary: Scale graph node size by the number of note links.
tasks.description: Calculate each note's degree from incoming and outgoing links and use that value to scale node radius in the D3 graph. Keep labels legible and avoid extreme sizing for highly connected notes.
acceptance_criteria: Nodes with more links render larger than sparsely connected nodes
Sizing uses note link count rather than a fixed radius
The graph remains readable and draggable after the size change
notes: This also closes the explicit planned item already called out in the design spec legend section.

tasks.title: Graph edge click drilldown
tasks.summary: Allow clicking an edge to inspect the two connected notes.
tasks.description: Make graph edges interactive so clicking a connection opens a lightweight detail state showing the source and target notes, with links to both note detail pages. Preserve existing pan, zoom, and node-click behavior.
acceptance_criteria: Clicking an edge reveals the two connected notes
Users can navigate from the edge drilldown to either note detail page
Edge interactions do not break node drag or node click navigation

tasks.title: Orphan note detection
tasks.summary: Surface notes with no incoming or outgoing links.
tasks.description: Add an orphan-note view or indicator so notes without graph connections can be identified quickly. The feature can live on the graph page, notes list, or a dedicated route, but it should be query-backed rather than manually inferred in the UI.
acceptance_criteria: Notes with no incoming and no outgoing links are identified correctly
Users can navigate from the orphan list or indicator to the note detail page
The orphan view updates as links are added or removed

tasks.title: Graph legend overlay
tasks.summary: Add a legend overlay to explain graph colours and node sizing.
tasks.description: Implement the legend overlay called out in the design spec so the graph communicates status colours and node-size meaning. If the graph gains category-colour mode, the legend should adapt to the active display mode.
acceptance_criteria: Legend is visible on the home graph without obscuring core interactions
Legend explains the active node colour mapping
Legend explains that larger nodes indicate more linked notes

tasks.title: Import notes from Markdown files
tasks.summary: Support batch import of note files with frontmatter into the database.
tasks.description: Implement a note import path that reads Markdown files with Techy frontmatter, validates them against the note schema, inserts or updates note rows, and syncs wikilinks after import. The import surface can be an authenticated route, action, or script-backed admin workflow.
acceptance_criteria: Markdown files with valid frontmatter can be imported in batch
Imported notes preserve title, tags, aliases, category, status, and body
Wikilinks are parsed and note_links are synced after import
Invalid files produce clear validation errors without corrupting the import batch
notes: Decide later whether the import entry point should be a UI form, a CLI script, or both.

tasks.title: Export all notes as a Markdown zip
tasks.summary: Export the full note library as Markdown files in a zip archive.
tasks.description: Add an authenticated export path that serializes each note to Markdown with frontmatter, bundles the files into a zip, and returns the archive for download. The export format should round-trip cleanly with the planned import workflow.
acceptance_criteria: Users can download a zip containing one Markdown file per note
Exported files include frontmatter and body content
The exported format is compatible with the planned import flow

tasks.title: Note history and revision tracking
tasks.summary: Add revision history so note changes can be inspected or recovered.
tasks.description: Introduce a revision strategy for note edits, either as soft delete plus historical snapshots or a dedicated revisions table. Capture prior note state on update and expose a minimal history view on the note detail or edit page.
acceptance_criteria: Editing a note preserves the previous version in a revision record
Users can inspect earlier revisions for a note
The chosen revision model is documented clearly
notes: If the chosen design changes the schema, add an ADR describing why.

tasks.title: Natural-language note lookup and grounded summary
tasks.summary: Implement POST /api/assistant/query to resolve an existing note and return a note-grounded summary.
tasks.description: Add the assistant query endpoint that takes a natural-language request such as 'tell me about SvelteKit', resolves the best matching note by title or alias, returns a note link, and produces a concise summary grounded in the saved note content.
acceptance_criteria: Natural-language queries can resolve a note by title or alias
Response returns matched note metadata including a note URL
Summary is grounded in the saved note content rather than a generic answer
Requests with no good match return a clear not-found response
notes: Prefer exact title and alias matches before falling back to partial matching.

tasks.title: Chat page assistant interface
tasks.summary: Wire the assistant experience into the dedicated /chat page and render grounded responses in the new conversation UI.
tasks.description: Implement the assistant chat interface on /chat. The page should accept natural-language questions, call the assistant query endpoint, and render the matched note link, grounded summary, possible additions, and new-topic ideas inside the dedicated chat layout rather than on the graph home page.
acceptance_criteria: User can submit a natural-language request from the /chat page
Response includes a clickable link to the matched note
Summary, possible additions, and new-topic ideas are visually separated
The interface feels native to the shared chat shell rather than bolted onto another page
notes: The dedicated chat page is now the primary assistant surface instead of an ask bar on the graph home page.

tasks.title: Assistant possible-additions suggestions
tasks.summary: Return suggested areas that may be missing or underdeveloped in the matched note.
tasks.description: Extend the assistant response so it can suggest likely additions for the current note. Suggestions must be framed as optional improvements or underdeveloped sections, not definitive claims that the note is wrong or incomplete.
acceptance_criteria: Assistant response includes a structured possibleGaps or equivalent field
Suggestions are phrased as additions or underdeveloped areas, not guaranteed omissions
Suggestions are derived from the current note context rather than returned as generic boilerplate

tasks.title: Next-note recommendations with de-duplication
tasks.summary: Return exactly 3 relevant topic ideas that do not already exist as note titles or aliases.
tasks.description: Add next-note recommendations to assistant responses and AI note-generation confirmations. Recommendations should be adjacent, worthwhile standalone topics, and must be filtered against existing titles and aliases so already-covered topics are excluded.
acceptance_criteria: Assistant response returns exactly 3 topic suggestions
Suggested topics are relevant to the matched or generated note
Suggestions do not duplicate existing note titles or aliases
Suggestions are clearly presented as candidates for new notes
notes: De-duplicate against both titles and aliases before finalizing the 3 suggestions.

tasks.title: Unified assistant respond endpoint with provider/model routing
tasks.summary: Replace the read-only assistant endpoint with a unified conversation endpoint that can return normal chat replies or structured mutation proposals.
tasks.description: Implement `POST /api/assistant/respond` as the primary assistant API. The endpoint should accept conversation messages, explicit UI mode (`chat` or `create`), provider, model, and optional topic cache state. Validate provider/model combinations, route requests through Anthropic or OpenAI adapters, and return a conversational assistant message plus an optional proposal payload. The provider/model registry should be structured so OpenRouter can be added later without changing the request contract.
acceptance_criteria: The chat UI can call `POST /api/assistant/respond` instead of `POST /api/assistant/query`
Invalid provider/model combinations return a clear 400 response
Anthropic and OpenAI are both supported through the same endpoint contract
The endpoint can return a plain conversational reply with no proposal
The endpoint can return a structured proposal payload when the assistant decides a note action is warranted
notes: The request contract should use provider/model pairs rather than hard-coding specific models into the chat UI.
Keep the interface compatible with a future OpenRouter adapter, but do not implement OpenRouter in this task.

tasks.title: Live web research integration and per-conversation topic cache
tasks.summary: Require live web research for assistant note creation and note comparison, while caching topic context within a single conversation.
tasks.description: Add a research layer to the unified assistant flow so create-note and compare/update work always uses live web research. Normalize source results into citation data for chat review, and reuse topic context already gathered in the active conversation so the same topic is not researched repeatedly in one session. Existing notes should still be loaded from the DB and compared against the newly researched context when relevant.
acceptance_criteria: Assistant create-note flows always perform live web research before returning a proposal
Assistant compare/update flows always perform live web research before deciding whether to propose a change
If the same topic is discussed again in the same conversation, the assistant reuses cached research context instead of re-running the same research step
Research citations are returned in assistant responses for review in chat
Citation data is not persisted into a dedicated database schema in this phase
notes: This task should not introduce a new citations table.
Cache scope is the active client-side chat session only.

tasks.title: Assistant commit endpoint for confirmed create/update/delete proposals
tasks.summary: Persist assistant proposals only after explicit confirmation from the chat UI.
tasks.description: Implement `POST /api/assistant/commit` to validate and apply confirmed assistant proposals for create, update, and delete operations. The endpoint should accept normalized proposal payloads from the chat UI, enforce proposal type validation, and delegate to the correct persistence flow. This becomes the mutation boundary for assistant-driven CRUD.
acceptance_criteria: Create, update, and delete proposals each have a validated commit path
Invalid proposal shapes return a clear 400 response
Create conflicts return a clear 409 response
Update and delete flows return 404 when the target note no longer exists
No assistant-driven note mutation happens until the commit endpoint is called
notes: This task establishes the mutation boundary; downstream tasks should plug their create/update/delete logic into this endpoint.

tasks.title: Assistant create-note persistence with immediate graph linking
tasks.summary: Persist confirmed assistant-created notes so graph connections appear immediately after save.
tasks.description: Implement the `create_note` commit path so a confirmed assistant draft inserts a new note row, writes AI provenance metadata, parses `[[wikilinks]]`, and syncs `note_links` immediately. The saved note should be visible in the graph without any follow-up action.
acceptance_criteria: Confirming a `create_note` proposal inserts a new note with title, body, tags, aliases, category, status, and AI metadata
The created note's `[[wikilinks]]` are parsed and synced to `note_links` during the same commit flow
The newly created note appears in the graph with its connections immediately after save
Duplicate title or slug conflicts return a clear error and do not create a partial note
notes: This task is only the created note itself; reciprocal patching of existing notes is tracked separately.

tasks.title: Reciprocal link propagation for assistant-created notes
tasks.summary: Allow assistant note creation to patch existing note bodies so new graph relationships are visible immediately in both directions where appropriate.
tasks.description: Extend the confirmed `create_note` flow so assistant proposals may include linked-note patches for existing notes that should now reference the newly created note. On commit, update those note bodies to include the new `[[Title]]` link, then re-sync their `note_links` rows in the same mutation sequence.
acceptance_criteria: Assistant create-note proposals can include one or more linked-note patches for existing notes
Confirming the create flow applies those body patches and re-syncs `note_links` for each affected note
The graph reflects both the new note's outgoing links and the patched existing notes' new links immediately after save
If any linked-note patch target is missing at commit time, the user receives a clear error and no partial patch set is applied
notes: The linked-note patches should be visible in chat review before the user confirms the mutation.

tasks.title: Update-mode prompt and respond endpoint
tasks.summary: Add update mode to the respond endpoint with a materiality-gated system prompt.
tasks.description: Add RESPOND_SYSTEM_PROMPT_UPDATE to prompts.ts that instructs the LLM to compare the saved note body against live research and only return an update_note proposal when the note is materially wrong, outdated, or substantially incomplete — not for minor or cosmetic differences. Extend /api/assistant/respond to accept mode:"update" with a noteId param; look up the saved note body from the DB and inject it into the prompt context. Extend respondConversation in claude.ts and chatgpt.ts to accept and forward the current note body. Update docs/API.md with the new mode and noteId param.
acceptance_criteria: Minor or cosmetic differences do not trigger an update_note proposal
Materially wrong, outdated, or substantially incomplete notes trigger an editable update_note proposal
mode:"update" with a valid noteId succeeds; missing/invalid noteId returns 400
npm run check passes with 0 errors
notes: commitUpdate in src/lib/server/assistant/commit.ts already snapshots revisions and re-syncs links — no changes needed there.
The materiality gate is purely a prompt instruction; do not add application-level diff logic.

tasks.title: Chat page update proposal UI
tasks.summary: Wire the update flow into the /chat page so users can select a note and review an update_note proposal.
tasks.description: Add an update review flow to the /chat page. The page.server.ts load function should expose a list of notes (id, title, slug) for selection. In the Svelte component, add a note-picker (a select or searchable input) that, when a note is chosen, sends a message to /api/assistant/respond with mode:"update" and the noteId. Display the returned update_note proposal in an editable panel (same draft-editor pattern as create_note). A confirm button calls /api/assistant/commit with the update_note proposal; on success show the slug link. Also handle the case where the assistant returns no proposal (no material issues found).
acceptance_criteria: User can select an existing note and trigger an update review from the chat page
The update_note proposal is displayed in an editable panel before confirmation
Confirming calls /api/assistant/commit and shows a success link
When no proposal is returned the assistant message explains why (no material issues)
npm run check passes with 0 errors
notes: Follow the existing create_note proposal panel pattern — do not invent new UI primitives.
The note-picker does not need to be fancy; a simple <select> is acceptable at this stage.

tasks.title: Assistant delete confirmation and commit flow
tasks.summary: Support assistant-driven note deletion behind an explicit confirmation step in chat.
tasks.description: Add `delete_note` proposal handling to the unified assistant routing and commit flow. Delete proposals should only appear for explicit delete intent, whether that intent comes from inference or an explicit override, and the UI should require a confirmation action before calling the commit endpoint. Once confirmed, the note should be deleted using the same cascade behavior already relied on by manual delete flows.
acceptance_criteria: Delete requests produce an explicit confirmation state in chat rather than deleting immediately
Confirming the delete removes the note and cascades its `note_links` rows
Cancelling the delete leaves the note untouched
Deleting a note through chat does not bypass existing not-found handling or safety checks
notes: Delete confirmation is a one-click confirm in chat, not a typed phrase.
Do not infer delete intent from ambiguous cleanup or edit requests.

tasks.title: Inference-first unified chat interface
tasks.summary: Turn `/chat` into the single assistant surface for conversation, note-aware routing, proposal review, and note mutations.
tasks.description: Redesign the `/chat` page so it supports standard conversation, provider/model selection, inline citations, matched-note context, compact create/update override controls, editable draft panels for create/update proposals, and confirmation UI for delete proposals. Replace the current summary-only assistant rendering with proposal-aware UI that can show resolved routing state, surface a related saved note without forcing mutation, and review or commit note changes inline.
acceptance_criteria: The chat UI remains one persistent composer and conversation surface rather than separate primary modes
The chat UI includes provider and model selectors plus compact create/update override controls near the composer
Strong existing-note matches render as inline context with follow-up research or review affordances without forcing an edit state
Create and update proposals render as editable inline draft panels before save
Delete proposals render as an explicit confirmation state
notes: This task replaces the legacy summary-oriented assistant message shape and should consume the new routing metadata returned by `/api/assistant/respond`.
Avoid separate mode tabs or a prominent create-mode switch as the primary mental model.

tasks.title: Remove dedicated new-note route and legacy creation entry points
tasks.summary: Remove `/notes/new` and clean up all product entry points that still assume a separate new-note page.
tasks.description: Delete the dedicated `/notes/new` route and remove `+ New` navigation, notes-page new-note buttons, and graph empty-state links that point to the legacy creation flow. Update copy and navigation so note authoring expectations point to `/chat` without introducing redirect-based create or update mode routes.
acceptance_criteria: The `/notes/new` route is removed from the app
Navigation no longer includes a `+ New` link
Notes-page and graph-page creation CTAs no longer point to `/notes/new`
Route cleanup includes running `npm run prepare` so generated route types stay current
notes: Do not add redirect-based create or update routes; note authoring should stay unified on `/chat`.

tasks.title: Tonal dark/light theme migration
tasks.summary: Replace the current three-theme model with a tonal dark/light system while keeping accent selection separate.
tasks.description: Rework the theme token system so `night`, `paper`, and `mist` are replaced by a true tonal `dark` / `light` model. Update defaults, localStorage persistence, nav controls, and any theme-dependent graph or component styles so the app clearly supports dark and light modes without hard black or harsh white surfaces.
acceptance_criteria: The app supports `dark` and `light` themes instead of `night`, `paper`, and `mist`
Accent selection remains independent and persists as before
The graph remains legible in both themes
No UI controls or persisted keys still depend on the removed three-theme naming
notes: Dark should remain tonal rather than pure black; light should remain tonal rather than stark white.

tasks.title: Melt UI primitives for assistant controls and review affordances
tasks.summary: Use Melt primitives where the new assistant interaction model benefits from better accessibility and keyboard behavior.
tasks.description: Adopt Melt UI primitives for the assistant controls that benefit from headless accessibility behavior: provider/model selectors, compact create/update override controls, matched-note disclosure affordances, proposal review surfaces, and compact confirmation interactions. Preserve Techy's custom visual language while improving behavior and keyboard interaction.
acceptance_criteria: Provider/model selectors use accessible Melt-backed behavior
Override controls and matched-note disclosure affordances are keyboard-usable and accessible
Proposal review and confirmation affordances are keyboard-usable and accessible
The assistant UI keeps Techy's custom styling rather than inheriting a canned component theme
The migration is limited to assistant-related controls rather than broad repo-wide refactors
notes: Prefer Melt only where interaction quality matters; avoid unnecessary churn in stable browsing/detail screens.

tasks.title: GSAP motion polish for assistant workflow
tasks.summary: Add restrained motion to the assistant experience where it improves continuity and focus.
tasks.description: Use GSAP selectively to polish the assistant workflow: proposal panel reveal/collapse, message/proposal transitions, delete confirmation state changes, and any subtle theme transition choreography that supports clarity. Keep the motion system scoped and avoid decorative animation loops.
acceptance_criteria: Assistant proposal panels and confirmation states animate in a restrained, readable way
Motion improves continuity without delaying core interactions
The implementation respects reduced-motion preferences
GSAP use stays scoped to meaningful assistant/theme transitions rather than broad page-wide animation
notes: This task should come after the structural chat UI and dark/light theme migration so the motion layer is polishing the intended final states.

tasks.title: Adopt Space Mono as the authoritative project font
tasks.summary: Make Space Mono the primary typeface across the app and align theme tokens and docs around that decision.
tasks.description: Replace the current Inter/Fira split with Space Mono as the canonical UI and editorial font across the shared app shell and page surfaces. Update font loading, theme tokens, and any component assumptions that still hardcode the old typography model.
acceptance_criteria: Space Mono is loaded and used as the default app font
Global font tokens no longer default to Inter or Fira Code
Nav, notes, graph shell, and note cards visually inherit the new typography without one-off overrides
Relevant design and product docs are updated to reflect the new typography authority
notes: Keep the font change global and authoritative rather than mixing old and new systems.

tasks.title: Redesign the shared app shell into a collapsible left rail
tasks.summary: Replace the current sticky top nav with a route-aware left rail that can collapse, auto-tuck, and expand without compromising the graph page.
tasks.description: Rework the shared `(app)` layout and navigation into a left sidebar inspired by the repository mockup. The rail should feel integrated rather than boxed, support a collapsed or tucked state on immersive pages like the graph, and use Melt where behavior and accessibility matter for disclosure, collapse, and compact controls.
acceptance_criteria: The app shell uses a left rail instead of the current top nav
The rail can collapse or auto-tuck and be expanded again without losing keyboard accessibility
The graph page remains low-chrome and visually close to its current immersive feel
Theme, accent, and account controls are integrated into the new rail cleanly
notes: Use Melt for interaction primitives, not for canned styling.
Prefer route-aware default states so the graph can stay tucked while notes can be expanded.

tasks.title: Redesign the notes page as the unified repository and search surface
tasks.summary: Merge browsing and search into `/notes` with a repository-style layout, integrated search bar, and mixed-density note presentation.
tasks.description: Replace the current notes grid plus separate search page model with a single `/notes` experience that follows the reference composition: large page title, integrated search bar, lighter category filtering, and a notes layout that avoids turning every result into the same boxed card. Use the current stack pragmatically and only introduce Melt where it improves filter, disclosure, or responsive behavior.
acceptance_criteria: The notes page includes the primary search input directly in the page header area
Title, tag, category, and orphan filtering are available from `/notes` without navigating to `/search`
The page composition feels closer to the reference and avoids a wall of identical component boxes
The layout works on desktop and mobile and coexists with the new left rail
notes: Phase 1 can keep filtering client-side against the notes already loaded by `/notes`.
Preserve import and export, but subordinate them to the repository header rather than button-heavy chrome.

tasks.title: Retire the dedicated search route and clean up navigation and docs
tasks.summary: Remove `/search` once `/notes` fully absorbs search responsibilities and sync the remaining product and documentation references.
tasks.description: Delete the standalone `/search` page, remove its navigation entry, and update product, API, and architecture docs so they describe `/notes` as the unified browsing and search surface. Ensure there are no stale links or route assumptions left in the shell after the notes redesign lands.
acceptance_criteria: The `/search` route files are removed
Navigation no longer links to a dedicated search page
Docs no longer describe `/search` as a first-class route
Generated route types are refreshed after route removal
notes: If any temporary redirect is kept during migration, it should be treated as short-lived cleanup rather than a permanent surface.

tasks.title: Unify graph overlays into a collapsible control panel
tasks.summary: Replace the separate graph legend and filter overlays with one bottom-right control panel that stays collapsed by default and expands on demand.
tasks.description: Rework the home graph controls in `ForceGraph.svelte` so the current legend, colour-mode toggle, and category/status filters live inside one Obsidian-inspired floating panel. The panel should sit in the bottom-right corner of the graph, show a compact trigger when collapsed, expand without taking over the page, and preserve the graph-first low-chrome feel. Edge drilldown remains a separate interaction state and should not be absorbed into the control panel.
acceptance_criteria: The graph shows one bottom-right controls trigger instead of separate legend and filter overlays
The control panel is collapsed by default and expands when invoked
Current colour-mode and category/status filter behavior remains available inside the unified panel
Removing the old overlays does not break graph drag, zoom, node navigation, or edge drilldown
notes: The panel should feel like a soft tuning dock rather than a permanent sidebar or utility drawer.
Keep the control surface compatible with the future route-aware left rail rather than hardcoding assumptions around the current top nav.

tasks.title: Persist graph view settings and add reset-to-defaults behavior
tasks.summary: Store graph tuning and filter preferences in local browser state and let the panel restore a documented default preset.
tasks.description: Add a client-owned graph settings model for the home graph and persist it in `localStorage` under a dedicated key. The stored values should include the unified panel's visual, filter, colour-mode, and force-layout settings, restore safely on reload, and expose a reset action that returns the graph to the documented Obsidian-like default preset. The implementation should validate or sanitize malformed stored values and must not introduce database-backed user settings or new API contracts.
acceptance_criteria: Graph control settings persist across reloads in the browser
Reset to defaults restores the documented graph preset from inside the control panel
Malformed or missing stored settings fall back safely without breaking graph render
No new schema, route, or API persistence is introduced for graph preferences
notes: Expanded/collapsed panel state is transient UI state and should not be persisted alongside graph settings.

tasks.title: Add Obsidian-style graph tuning controls for appearance and physics
tasks.summary: Expose live graph tuning controls for node sizing, line thickness, text fade, and D3 force behavior inside the unified panel.
tasks.description: Extend the unified graph control panel so it can tune the existing D3 graph live without forcing a full redraw unless a specific simulation parameter requires reheating. The first version should include node-size scaling on top of the existing degree-based sizing rule, visible link thickness control, zoom-threshold text fade behavior, and a dense but D3-native physics set covering link distance, charge strength, collision spacing, centering strength, velocity decay, and alpha decay. Preserve graph readability, drag behavior, zooming, and edge hit-area usability while deferring any expert-only Obsidian controls that do not map cleanly to the current D3 simulation.
acceptance_criteria: Users can tune node scale, visible link thickness, and text fade threshold from the graph panel
Users can tune D3-native force controls including link distance, repulsion, spacing, centering, and simulation decay values
Node sizing remains degree-based with a user-controlled multiplier rather than becoming fully manual
Graph interactions remain intact and edge hit zones stay usable even when visible line thickness changes
notes: Prefer direct mappings to existing D3 force parameters; if a broader Obsidian-style control would require disproportionate custom behavior, defer it rather than expanding scope inside this task.

tasks.title: Canonical note taxonomy enforcement across server write paths
tasks.summary: Centralize the canonical category list and enforce it consistently for assistant commit, manual note create/edit, and Markdown import flows.
tasks.description: Add a shared note-taxonomy module that exposes the canonical category list and category normalization/validation helpers described in `docs/NOTES.md`. Use that shared taxonomy at every server-side note write boundary: assistant commit, manual create/edit actions, and Markdown import. Write paths should reject or clearly surface invalid categories instead of persisting ad hoc labels, while still allowing tags to remain open-vocabulary. Keep the persistence shape unchanged for now (`category` stays `text`), but make the application contract strict and consistent everywhere note data enters the system.
acceptance_criteria: There is one shared source of truth for canonical note categories used by application code
Assistant commit rejects non-canonical categories instead of persisting them
Manual create/edit and import flows no longer accept arbitrary category strings silently
Tags remain flexible and are not converted into a fixed enum or fixed taxonomy
npm run check passes with 0 errors
notes: Prefer a shared helper or constants module over duplicating the category list inside multiple route files.
This task is enforcement only; do not mix in UI selector redesign or historical data cleanup.

tasks.title: Controlled category selection in note authoring surfaces
tasks.summary: Replace free-text category entry in manual note authoring with a controlled selector backed by the canonical taxonomy.
tasks.description: Update the manual note create and edit surfaces so category selection is driven by the canonical category list rather than a free-text text input. The UI should make the primary-category choice explicit, preserve the existing lightweight form feel, and avoid turning tags into a second category mechanism. Reuse the shared taxonomy introduced by the enforcement task rather than duplicating options in the Svelte components.
acceptance_criteria: The create and edit note forms use a controlled category selector instead of free-text category inputs
The selector options match the canonical categories exactly
Submitting the form preserves the selected canonical category without UI-only remapping logic
The form still allows flexible tag entry and existing tag suggestions
notes: A native <select> is acceptable for the first pass; do not overbuild a searchable combobox unless the current form density truly requires it.

tasks.title: Assistant taxonomy context and tag-reuse grounding
tasks.summary: Ground assistant note proposals in the canonical category list and the graph's current tag vocabulary so the model reuses tags instead of inventing near-duplicates.
tasks.description: Extend the assistant create/update prompt pipeline so it is not limited to static instructions alone. The respond flow should provide the LLM with the canonical category list and a compact view of already-used tags from the graph, then instruct the model to prefer existing lower-case tags when they fit and only mint a new tag when no current tag is a clean match. Keep the context lightweight and deterministic so prompt size does not grow unbounded as the graph expands.
acceptance_criteria: Assistant create and update flows receive the canonical category list from shared application state rather than hard-coded prompt duplicates
Assistant prompts receive a bounded list of existing graph tags for reuse guidance
Prompt instructions explicitly prefer existing lower-case tags before creating new ones
The added grounding does not change the external respond endpoint contract
notes: Do not dump every tag in the database into the prompt if the list becomes large; bound or summarize it deterministically.

tasks.title: Inference-first assistant intent routing and note matching
tasks.summary: Resolve conversational, create, and review/update behavior on the server from the transcript and optional overrides instead of requiring the client to choose a mode on every turn.
tasks.description: Extend the assistant respond flow so the server resolves whether a turn is conversational, create-oriented, or review/update-oriented from the transcript and optional UI overrides rather than requiring the client to choose a mode for every turn. Add a shared routing layer that keeps legacy `mode` support during migration, inspects the latest user turn, performs conservative exact-title and alias matching against existing notes, and returns normalized routing metadata for the UI. Pure learning prompts about an existing note topic should stay conversational first, while explicit create or update overrides continue to force the intended branch.
acceptance_criteria: Server-side routing resolves intent when no explicit override is provided
Legacy `mode` input remains supported as a temporary compatibility alias for the override contract
Conservative note matching only auto-targets strong exact title or alias matches; weaker matches do not silently become updates
The response payload includes routing metadata the UI can use to show matched-note context and override state
notes: Prefer exact title and alias matching over fuzzy semantic search for the first pass.
Keep routing deterministic and debuggable; do not hide the resolved routing state from the client.

tasks.title: Unified prompt assembly and note-aware conversational behavior
tasks.summary: Refactor assistant prompt construction so one shared assistant identity can converse, create, review, and delete through internal skills without exposing separate primary modes.
tasks.description: Refactor the assistant prompt pipeline so chat, create, update, and delete are treated as internal skills on top of one shared assistant identity. Introduce note-aware conversational behavior for strong existing-note matches so the assistant can summarize what is already saved and offer further research or review without forcing an update proposal. Preserve the materiality gate for update proposals, keep delete proposals limited to explicit delete intent, and ensure proposal-first behavior remains intact across providers.
acceptance_criteria: Prompt assembly starts from one shared assistant identity with routed skill-specific instructions layered on top
A prompt like `teach me about Django` stays conversational even when a `Django` note exists, while surfacing a related-note affordance or follow-up offer
Update proposals remain materiality-gated and proposal-first, and delete proposals require explicit delete intent
Anthropic and OpenAI adapters continue to return the same normalized assistant message and proposal shape after the prompt refactor
notes: Do not remove explicit override support in this task; the prompt layer consumes routed intent rather than replacing the UI control contract.
Delete intent stays explicit even under inference-first routing.

tasks.title: Standardized note-section prompt contract
tasks.summary: Require assistant-generated note drafts to follow the approved note section skeleton and optional-section rules from docs/NOTES.md.
tasks.description: Extend the unified assistant create/update prompt contract so generated note drafts follow the standardized note section skeleton from `docs/NOTES.md`. The prompt should require `Overview`, `Description`, `Key Concepts`, `Connections`, and `Resources` in that order, allow only the approved optional sections in the documented order, keep `Overview` brief, treat `Description` as the primary deep-explanation section, and discourage deprecated default headings such as `Current Status`, `Notable Features`, `Quick Examples`, and `Industry Usage`.
acceptance_criteria: Create-note prompts explicitly require the standardized note section skeleton
Update-note prompts normalize replacement drafts toward the same structure
Prompt instructions ban the deprecated default headings
Prompt guidance prefers evergreen explanation over release-churn unless `Version Notes` is warranted
notes: Keep the note-shape rules in one shared prompt contract so create and update behavior do not drift.
Do not force topic-specific optional sections when they do not materially improve the note.

tasks.title: Backfill existing note categories to the canonical taxonomy
tasks.summary: Normalize already-saved note categories to the new canonical list and surface any rows that still need manual review.
tasks.description: Add a one-off normalization path for existing note rows so category drift already in the database is corrected, not just future writes. The backfill should map obvious legacy values such as `Web Frameworks`, `Databases`, `DevOps & CI/CD`, and similar near-duplicates to the canonical replacements from `docs/NOTES.md`, apply safe updates in the database, and report any ambiguous or unmapped categories for manual cleanup rather than guessing silently. Keep this as an explicit operator-run cleanup step, not an automatic runtime mutation.
acceptance_criteria: There is a documented repo-local way to run the category backfill intentionally
Obvious legacy categories are updated to their canonical replacements
Ambiguous or unknown categories are reported for manual review rather than silently rewritten
The backfill does not change tags or note bodies as part of category cleanup
notes: Prefer an explicit script or command over embedding one-off cleanup logic into normal request handlers.

tasks.title: Assistant note-body structure enforcement
tasks.summary: Validate assistant create/update note bodies against the standardized section skeleton before persistence.
tasks.description: Add shared server-side note-body structure validation for assistant-driven create and update commits so malformed or deprecated note section layouts are rejected or normalized before persistence. The enforcement should verify the required section order, allow only the approved optional sections between `Key Concepts` and `Connections`, and prevent assistant proposals from persisting banned default headings while leaving manual note editing behavior unchanged unless explicitly routed through assistant commit.
acceptance_criteria: Assistant `create_note` commits enforce the required note section skeleton
Assistant `update_note` commits enforce the same section rules on replacement drafts
Deprecated headings are rejected or normalized before persistence
Manual note CRUD routes remain unchanged unless they use the assistant commit boundary
notes: Enforce structure at the assistant mutation boundary first; do not broaden manual note-edit validation in this pass.

tasks.title: Persist app-owned chat conversations and messages
tasks.summary: Add lean database-backed chat history storage for conversations and messages without persisting provider-side hidden memory or raw research payloads.
tasks.description: Implement the persistence layer for chat continuity by adding the planned conversation/message storage and wiring the assistant runtime to save canonical transcript records. The stored data should be app-owned and lean: conversation metadata, user/assistant messages, lightweight citations, and proposal snapshots when present. Do not persist provider-specific conversation IDs, hidden memory handles, raw live-research payloads, or the ephemeral topic cache.
acceptance_criteria: Database-backed chat conversation and chat message storage exists for app-owned transcript data
Assistant turns persist canonical message records with lightweight citation and proposal snapshot data when present
Provider-managed hidden memory identifiers and raw live-research payloads are not stored
The persistence shape stays lean enough for Neon free-tier usage
notes: Keep the stored transcript app-owned and canonical; do not model provider-specific hidden conversation state.
Persist only the data required to reopen the conversation and rebuild model context.

tasks.title: Chat history rail and resume UI
tasks.summary: Add the restrained recent-conversations rail or drawer described in the chat spec so saved threads can be reopened without competing with the active conversation.
tasks.description: Update the chat page layout to match the documented history pattern: a quiet recent-conversations rail or drawer, a visible new-chat affordance, and a conversation column that remains the primary surface. On desktop the history should read as a restrained notebook index; on mobile it should collapse into a drawer or sheet. The active-thread empty state should remain scoped to the conversation column and should not be confused with saved history.
acceptance_criteria: Desktop chat includes a restrained recent-conversations rail or drawer with a clear new-chat affordance
Mobile chat collapses saved history into a compact drawer or sheet rather than permanently consuming space
Selecting a saved conversation restores its transcript while keeping the current thread as the dominant surface
The conversation empty state copy is clearly about the active thread, not the saved history area
notes: Keep chat history low-noise and subordinate to the active conversation.
Implement this against the simplified composer-led chat shell from the follow-on UI tasks rather than the current heavy header layout.

tasks.title: Simplify chat shell and empty-state layout
tasks.summary: Remove the current cluttered chat header and boxed empty-state card in favor of a minimal centered entry state.
tasks.description: Refactor the top-level `/chat` presentation so it follows the calmer reference direction documented for chat. Remove the current eyebrow, descriptive lede, and summary pills. Replace the `No conversation yet` explainer card with a centered empty state that keeps only minimal Techy branding/title above the composer. The conversation area should feel open rather than wrapped in a generic dashboard shell.
acceptance_criteria: The current eyebrow, descriptive lede, and summary pills are removed from the top of the chat page
The `No conversation yet` component and its explainer cards are removed
Empty chat presents a centered minimal entry state above the composer rather than a boxed instructional card
The main chat surface is not wrapped in a generic component shell for this pass
notes: Use `references/UI/chat-example.png` as the layout-vibe reference and `references/UI/current-chat.png` as the before-state.
Translate the reference through Techy's token system and typography instead of copying another product literally.

tasks.title: Composer-integrated chat controls
tasks.summary: Move mode and model controls into the composer chrome so chat reads like a modern AI surface while staying inference-first.
tasks.description: Replace the current separate provider/model row and override button group with composer-integrated controls. `Auto`, `Create`, and `Update` should become compact skill-like chips attached to the prompt box. The initial selected mode should remain `Auto`. Keep the model picker directly visible in the composer chrome, but demote provider switching to a quieter secondary control. When `Update` is active, show the note target picker inline under the composer before send. Preserve the current provider-registry defaults rather than introducing new hardcoded model defaults.
acceptance_criteria: The initial selected mode is `Auto` when `/chat` loads
`Auto`, `Create`, and `Update` render as compact skill-like controls integrated with the composer
The visible model selector stays in the composer chrome and still initializes from the current server-supplied provider defaults
Provider switching remains available but no longer occupies equal visual weight with the model control
Choosing `Update` reveals an inline note target picker anchored to the composer before send
notes: Do not change the current provider default or provider-specific model defaults in this task.
Keep the control treatment compact enough that the composer remains the primary focal point.

tasks.title: Flatten chat transcript and inline review styling
tasks.summary: Replace the current rounded-card transcript look with a flatter, calmer conversation treatment that keeps proposals inline.
tasks.description: Restyle the active chat transcript so the routine conversation flow no longer reads like stacked rounded components. User and assistant messages should feel flatter and more editorial, while citations, matched-note affordances, and proposal panels remain inline and readable. Keep the proposal workflow in the route surface and do not move the core chat experience into a new wrapper component in this pass.
acceptance_criteria: Routine chat messages no longer use the current heavy rounded component treatment
Inline proposals, citations, and matched-note affordances remain visible and usable within the thread
The transcript styling aligns with the calmer composer-led shell from the preceding chat refactor tasks
The main chat experience remains implemented in the route surface rather than being re-boxed into a wrapper component
notes: Keep proposal editing inline; this task changes presentation, not the proposal workflow boundary.

tasks.title: 21st.dev composer static layout
tasks.summary: Refine the current composer-integrated chat controls so the default prompt box closely matches the 21st.dev EaseMize static reference.
tasks.description: Refine the already composer-led `/chat` UI so its default static state closely matches the 21st.dev EaseMize AI Prompt Box reference at https://21st.dev/community/components/easemize/ai-prompt-box/default while staying within Techy's token system, typography, current icons, and assistant API contract. Focus this task on visual structure only: rounded prompt surface, textarea position, bottom action row, compact control density, active pill footprint, subtle action dividers, and far-right circular send control. The prompt box should look almost 1:1 with the reference, but it must only expose current Techy controls: Auto/Create/Update, provider, model, selected update note, and send. Do not add upload, voice, canvas, image preview, stop-generation affordances, Melt rewrites, or GSAP animation in this task.
acceptance_criteria: The default `/chat` composer static layout closely reflects the 21st.dev prompt-box reference while using Techy tokens and local icon treatment
The prompt box has a textarea region above a compact bottom action row with subtle dividers and a far-right circular send control
`Auto`, `Create`, and `Update` remain visible in the composer region as current controls only
Provider and model controls remain available but visually secondary to the prompt and send action
No new unsupported reference affordances such as upload, voice, canvas, image preview, or stop generation are shown
notes: Treat the 21st.dev component as a visual and interaction reference, not as code to import.
Inspect the 21st.dev live page/source/registry before implementing so the static state is nearly 1:1 within Techy's palette, type, and current control constraints.
Keep this pass visual and structural; later tasks own behavior hardening, Melt primitives, motion, and QA.

tasks.title: Composer control contract preservation
tasks.summary: Verify and harden the current assistant controls after the 21st.dev static composer layout is applied.
tasks.description: After UI-021 moves the visible controls into the 21st.dev-inspired prompt box, verify and harden the behavioral contract for the current controls. `Auto`, `Create`, and `Update` must preserve the existing assistant request behavior and payload shape, provider/model selection must still initialize from server-supplied defaults, Update mode must keep the selected-note requirement before send, and send should remain disabled for empty prompts or missing update targets. This task should avoid visual churn except where needed to make states readable within the new composer layout.
acceptance_criteria: `Auto`, `Create`, and `Update` preserve the existing assistant mode/override behavior and do not change the `/api/assistant/respond` request contract
Provider and model values still initialize from `+page.server.ts` data and update correctly when provider changes
Update mode reveals the selected-note control and disables send until a target note is selected
Empty prompts and loading states keep the send control disabled as expected
No unsupported upload, voice, canvas, image preview, or stop-generation behavior is introduced
notes: Keep behavior compatible with the existing assistant endpoints; this task should not introduce API or schema changes.
Prefer small state and event-handler fixes over another layout pass.

tasks.title: Melt composer accessibility primitives
tasks.summary: Use Melt where useful to make the 21st.dev-inspired composer controls keyboard accessible without changing Techy's visual language.
tasks.description: Layer Melt Svelte 5 primitives onto the composer controls from UI-021 and UI-022 where they improve accessibility and interaction quality. The exclusive `Auto`/`Create`/`Update` control should behave like a single-choice control even if it visually resembles skill chips. Provider/model controls should use Melt or another accessible headless treatment where native browser menus break theme contrast, focus, or keyboard behavior. Compact tooltips or disclosures may use Melt when useful, but the visual styling must continue to match the 21st.dev reference through Techy tokens rather than adopting Melt demo styling.
acceptance_criteria: `Auto`, `Create`, and `Update` have accessible single-choice semantics, keyboard behavior, and visible focus states
Provider/model controls remain keyboard accessible and themed consistently within the composer chrome
Any tooltip or disclosure behavior is accessible and does not add persistent clutter
The visual layout from UI-021 remains recognizably aligned with the 21st.dev prompt-box reference
The implementation passes `npm run check` without adding React, Radix React, `framer-motion`, or `lucide-react`
notes: Before implementing, inspect the installed `melt` package examples/types or current Melt docs for Svelte 5 `RadioGroup`, `Toggle`, `Tooltip`, and `Select` usage.
Melt should provide headless behavior only; match the reference through Techy CSS tokens rather than adopting Melt demo styling.

tasks.title: GSAP composer motion parity
tasks.summary: Add restrained 21st.dev-style active-control motion to the composer using GSAP or simpler CSS transitions.
tasks.description: Add motion to the 21st.dev-inspired composer after the static layout and accessible controls are stable. Reproduce the reference's Framer Motion-style active-control choreography with local tools: short icon scale/rotation, active label width/opacity, and smooth state changes. Use GSAP where it improves continuity and CSS transitions where they are simpler. Scope any GSAP animations to the Svelte component and clean them up on unmount or state changes. Do not add decorative loops, exaggerated hover animation, page-wide motion, or new unsupported composer actions.
acceptance_criteria: Active composer controls animate in a restrained 21st.dev-inspired way without causing layout instability
Icon scale/rotation and active label width/opacity transitions are smooth and do not obscure controls
Simple hover, border, and background changes remain CSS transitions where GSAP is unnecessary
Any GSAP animations are scoped to the component and cleaned up on unmount or state changes
Motion respects Techy's no decorative loops and no exaggerated hover guidance
notes: Before implementing, inspect GSAP documentation or Context7 examples for scoped Svelte-compatible animation cleanup.
Use GSAP as the local substitute for Framer Motion only where active-control choreography benefits from it.

tasks.title: Composer responsive visual QA
tasks.summary: Verify the 21st.dev-inspired composer across desktop and mobile states after layout, behavior, Melt, and motion work land.
tasks.description: Perform the final visual and responsive QA pass for the 21st.dev-inspired chat composer. Validate desktop and mobile layouts, long model names, provider/model control wrapping, Update note picker placement, empty-state fit, loading/send disabled states, active chip expansion, and transcript/composer spacing. Compare the final static state against the 21st.dev EaseMize reference and adjust only small spacing, sizing, and wrapping issues needed to preserve the almost-1:1 prompt-box feel within Techy's palette and current controls.
acceptance_criteria: Desktop composer layout remains visually close to the 21st.dev prompt-box reference
Mobile composer controls wrap or collapse without horizontal overflow or text clipping
Long provider/model/note labels do not break the action row or send button placement
Update, loading, empty prompt, and active mode states remain readable and stable
No unsupported reference actions or new dependencies are introduced during QA
notes: This is a polish and verification task, not a new design direction.
Use the 21st.dev reference as the visual target and Techy's docs as the constraint system.

tasks.title: Add conversation persistence schema and migration
tasks.summary: Add conversations and conversation_messages tables to the Drizzle schema and apply migration to Neon.
tasks.description: Add two new tables to schema.ts: conversations (id, userId, createdAt, updatedAt, title) and conversationMessages (id, conversationId, role, content, createdAt). Export TypeScript inference types. Run npm run db:generate then npm run db:migrate to apply. No other files should change.
acceptance_criteria: schema.ts exports conversations and conversationMessages table definitions
TypeScript types Conversation, NewConversation, ConversationMessage, NewConversationMessage are exported
Migration SQL is generated and applied to Neon without errors
npm run check passes
notes: Run db:generate then db:migrate after schema changes.
conversations.userId references users.id with cascade delete.
conversationMessages.conversationId references conversations.id with cascade delete.

tasks.title: Conversation server module — CRUD queries
tasks.summary: Create src/lib/server/assistant/conversations.ts with typed query functions for creating conversations, appending messages, loading a thread, and listing recent conversations.
tasks.description: Create the file src/lib/server/assistant/conversations.ts and export four functions: createConversation(userId, title?) → Conversation, appendMessages(conversationId, messages: {role, content}[]) → void, getConversation(conversationId, userId) → {conversation, messages} | null, listRecentConversations(userId, limit=20) → Conversation[]. All queries use the Drizzle db client from src/lib/server/db/index.ts. No route or page files change in this task.
acceptance_criteria: createConversation inserts a row into conversations and returns the new record
appendMessages inserts one row per message into conversationMessages
getConversation returns the conversation with its messages ordered by createdAt, or null if not found or not owned by userId
listRecentConversations returns conversations ordered by updatedAt desc
npm run check passes
notes: This is a pure server-side data module — no SvelteKit route concerns here.
Use eq, desc, and asc from drizzle-orm for query predicates.

tasks.title: Persist messages in respond API
tasks.summary: Update the /api/assistant/respond endpoint to create or continue a conversation in the DB and persist user + assistant messages on each exchange.
tasks.description: Modify src/routes/api/assistant/respond/+server.ts so that on each POST: (1) if no conversationId is provided in the request body, create a new conversation for the authenticated user; (2) append the incoming user message to the conversation; (3) after the assistant reply is generated, append the assistant message; (4) return conversationId in the JSON response alongside the existing reply fields. The request/response shape must remain backward-compatible — conversationId is an optional addition, not a replacement.
acceptance_criteria: A new conversation row is created when conversationId is absent from the request
User message is persisted before the assistant call
Assistant message is persisted after the response is generated
conversationId is included in the JSON response
Existing Auto/Create/Update mode behavior is unchanged
npm run check passes
notes: The endpoint already has session auth — use the session userId for conversation ownership.
Keep streaming or non-streaming behavior unchanged; persistence is a side-effect.

tasks.title: Resume conversations via routing and page load
tasks.summary: Wire up /chat to return recent conversations, add /chat/[conversationId] route, and update the page to rebuild the messages array from a loaded transcript.
tasks.description: Three changes: (1) update src/routes/(app)/chat/+page.server.ts load to call listRecentConversations and include results alongside existing provider/note data; (2) create src/routes/(app)/chat/[conversationId]/+page.server.ts that calls getConversation and returns conversation + messages (redirect to /chat if not found); (3) update src/routes/(app)/chat/+page.svelte to accept the conversations prop and, when a conversationId is present in page data, initialise the local messages state from the saved transcript so subsequent assistant calls continue from the right context. Run npm run prepare after adding the new route.
acceptance_criteria: /chat load returns recent conversation metadata alongside the existing provider and note data
/chat/[conversationId] loads conversation metadata plus saved messages for the selected thread
Resuming a conversation rebuilds the assistant request transcript from saved messages
Starting a new chat from /chat remains possible without deleting saved conversations
npm run prepare and npm run check both pass
notes: Run npm run prepare after creating the new [conversationId] route.
The page already manages a local messages array — initialise it from data.messages when present.
